text: Hello, Adam Rothermel here again. Just posting a pretty routine outpatient robotic-assisted cholecystectomy. Nothing really special about this case, honestly. Favorable patient, body habitus, favorable anatomy. I'm honestly just trying to get better at editing videos and gaining the confidence to post videos. So any feedback would be very greatly appreciated.

The only slightly interesting thing about this case was that the patient had kind of a posterior cystic artery branch that I took a little extra time to follow up and make sure it was going into the gallbladder and not diving back into the liver before sacrificing it. So I hope you enjoy. Thank you.
